sort_int_correct.solution:
Proof.
  induction al as [| h t IH].
  - simpl. split. constructor. constructor.
  - simpl. destruct IH as [IHperm IHsorted]. split.
    + apply perm_trans with (h :: sort_int t).
      * apply perm_skip. assumption.
      * clear IHperm IHsorted. induction (sort_int t) as [| h' t' IH'].
        -- simpl. apply Permutation_refl.
        -- simpl. bdestruct (leb h h').
           ++ apply Permutation_refl.
           ++ apply perm_trans with (h' :: h :: t').
              ** apply perm_swap.
              ** apply perm_skip. apply IH'.
    + clear IHperm. induction IHsorted.
      * simpl. constructor.
      * simpl. bdestruct (leb h (Abs x)).
        -- constructor; auto. constructor.
        -- constructor; auto. lia. constructor.
      * simpl. bdestruct (leb h (Abs x)).
        -- constructor; auto. simpl in IHIHsorted.
           bdestruct (leb h (Abs y)).
           ++ constructor; auto.
           ++ constructor; auto. lia.
        -- simpl in IHIHsorted. bdestruct (leb h (Abs y)).
           ++ constructor; auto. lia.
           ++ constructor; auto. lia.
Qed.
sort_int_correct.explanation: Adapts the insertion sort correctness proof from Sort to work with int keys and Abs comparisons. Uses leb instead of <=?.

lookup_insert_eq.solution:
Proof.
  induction t; intros; bdall.
Qed.
lookup_insert_eq.explanation: By induction on t using the bdall tactic to handle all boolean destructions.

lookup_insert_neq.solution:
Proof.
  induction t; intros; bdall.
  all: try (apply Abs_inj in e; contradiction).
Qed.
lookup_insert_neq.explanation: By induction on t using bdall. Uses Abs_inj to resolve contradictions when keys are claimed equal.

int_elements.solution: (* Optional 4-star exercise - port BST definition and elements properties for int-keyed trees *)
int_elements.explanation: This optional exercise asks to port the BST definition and reprove elements properties for int-keyed trees.
